sections.paragraph:
(prob. “whom, or what God sets free,” see the root פָּתַח Isa 14:17), pr.n.-

(1) [Jiphtah], a town in the tribe of Judah, Jos 15:43.-(2) [Jephthah], a judge of Israel, celebrated for having, in compliance with a vow, sacrificed his daughter, Jud. 11:12 1Sa 12:11 Greek Ἰεφθα, Ἰεφθάε, Vulg. Jephte.
